title: Storyboard · "Knock" — Radio→Animation production script · S3
body:
**Scene 3: 02:00–03:00 · Walter at the Window**

HERO FRAME: Interior of a small 1949 apartment, evening · Walter from behind, silhouetted against a window overlooking the empty street · a single brass lamp lit · a coffee cup on the side table · a wool jacket over the back of the chair · NO FACE VISIBLE

MOTION: kling — Walter's shoulders rise and fall once with a slow breath. The lamp's bulb flickers gently. Curtain stirs. The coffee cup's surface ripples once from a vibration far below the street.

MOOD: Confessional.

AUDIO: Walter: *"My name is Walter Phelan. I am a man of forty-three years. Until eight weeks ago I was an accountant for the Crawford Mills Furniture Company. Now I am an accountant for nobody, because there is nobody left to keep books for."*

source.label: Dimension X episode #05 (NBC Radio, May 6, 1950) adapting Fredric Brown's 1948 short-short story "Knock" (originally in *Thrilling Wonder Stories*, December 1948)

author: matt

witnesses.label: Rufinus of Aquileia, Commentary on the Apostles' Creed (c. AD 404)
Pirminius, De singulis libris canonicis scarapsus (c. AD 750) — earliest received form
Cited by every major catechism since 8th century
